prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde (H₂C=O) were computed at the DFT level using the B3LYP functional with the def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard'), in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, via PySCF (chemkit DFT engine). The N−1 (cation, charge +1, mult 2) and N+1 (anion, charge −1, mult 2) states were also computed to form the finite-difference Fukui functions. Charges were partitioned by the Mulliken scheme.

Geometry: user-supplied .xyz file at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz; single-point (no re-optimization).

Per-atom Fukui indices (Mulliken, B3LYP/def2-TZVP, gas phase):

  Atom 1 — O:  f+ = 0.46814,  f− = 0.46742,  f0 = 0.46778,  Δf = +0.000714
  Atom 2 — C:  f+ = 0.70839,  f− = 0.11244,  f0 = 0.41042,  Δf = +0.59595
  Atom 3 — H:  f+ = −0.08827, f− = 0.21008,  f0 = 0.06090,  Δf = −0.29835
  Atom 4 — H:  f+ = −0.08826, f− = 0.21006,  f0 = 0.06090,  Δf = −0.29832

Most electrophilic site (largest f+): C (atom 2), f+ = 0.70839 — primary site for nucleophilic attack, consistent with carbonyl reactivity.
Most nucleophilic site (largest f−): O (atom 1), f− = 0.46742 — primary site for electrophilic attack.

Charge conservation: Σf+ = 1.0000, Σf− = 1.0000. All integrity checks passed. No warnings reported. Integrity: trustworthy = true.

The headline value reported is the maximum f+ index (carbonyl carbon, atom 2) = 0.70839.
